Jurassic World (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $653,406,625Details
International Box Office $1,017,657,016
Worldwide Box Office $1,671,063,641
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $53,572,785
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $96,822,734
Total $150,395,519
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Visitors at the Jurassic World theme park run for their lives when the genetically engineered Indominus Rex and other dinosaurs go on a rampage.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$208,806,270 (32.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:39.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$215,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,274 opening theaters/4,291 max. theaters, 8.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $926,740,059

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 12th, 2015 (Wide) by Universal
June 12th, 2015 (IMAX) by Universal
January 8th, 2021 (Limited) by Universal
International Releases: June 9th, 2015 (Wide) (China)
June 10th, 2015 (Wide) (France)
June 10th, 2015 (Wide) (Philippines)
June 10th, 2015 (Wide) (Belgium)
June 10th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
Video Release: October 20th, 2015 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of science-fiction violence and peril.
(Rating bulletin 2373, 5/13/2015)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Franchise: Jurassic Park
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell, Directing Yourself, Director Cameo, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Legacy Sequel, Motion Capture Performance
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): New Orleans, LA
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (locale): NASA Michoud Assembly Facility, New Orleans, Six Flags New Orleands, LA
Filming location (state or county): Hawaii, United States, Kauai, HI, Louisiana, United States, Oahu, HI
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad, Children Dealing with Divorce, Cloning, Dinosaurs, Dinosaurs in the Modern World, Disaster, Dysfunctional Family, Escape, Military Stealing New Invention, Underwater
Social setting: Navy, On Tour
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1990s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Amblin Entertainment, Legendary Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Visitors at the Jurassic World theme park run for their lives when the genetically engineered Indominus Rex and other dinosaurs go on a rampage.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$208,806,270 (32.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:39.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$215,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,274 opening theaters/4,291 max. theaters, 8.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $926,740,059

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 12th, 2015 (Wide) by Universal
June 12th, 2015 (IMAX) by Universal
January 8th, 2021 (Limited) by Universal
International Releases: June 9th, 2015 (Wide) (China)

Video Release: October 20th, 2015 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of science-fiction violence and peril.
(Rating bulletin 2373, 5/13/2015)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Franchise: Jurassic Park
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell, Directing Yourself, Director Cameo, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Legacy Sequel, Motion Capture Performance
